_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox host Phillies in battle of struggling teams with new managers
items.snark: Two teams who nuked their managers in April meet at Fenway, proving sometimes everyone just needs a fresh scapegoat.

items.headline: Red Sox Minor Lines: Anthony Eyanson makes first Double-A start, Franklin Arias rakes
items.snark: Eyanson tossed four innings of one-run ball in his Double-A debut, making the Payton Tolle speedrun to the majors look repeatable.

items.headline: Red Sox offense hits unacceptable low: worst home OPS since 1968
items.snark: This home OPS would be the worst by a Red Sox team since 1968, which is to say before the DH and modern baseball existed.

items.headline: Caleb Durbin not letting struggles at the plate affect his Gold Glove defense
items.snark: Durbin is hitting .173 but fielding like Matt Chapman, which is like being the hottest guy at the DMV.

items.headline: Kyle Williams sticking with jersey number 18, reverses switch to number 8
items.snark: Williams changed his mind on switching to Stefon Diggs' old number, which is either superstition or a bad omen for 2026.

items.headline: Patriots sign pair of tryout players Xavier Holmes and Peter Manuma after minicamp
items.snark: Two tryout guys landed deals after rookie camp, giving Patriots Twitter nine months to insist one is the next Malcolm Butler.

items.headline: Patriots face 12th-easiest 2026 schedule despite Super Bowl hangover worries
items.snark: Sharp Football says the Pats have the 12th-easiest schedule, which feels generous given they face the NFC North.

items.headline: Patriots rookie Caleb Lomu takes all left tackle reps at Saturday minicamp
items.snark: First-rounder Lomu exclusively repping at left tackle all weekend tells you everything about Will Campbell's future at right.

items.headline: Patriots rookies share fun facts at minicamp: Jam Miller engaged, Karon Prunty has five sisters
items.snark: Rookie introductions included Prunty admitting five older sisters meant racing for the bathroom, which tracks for a defensive back.

items.headline: Patriots schedule release set for Thursday May 14 at 8 PM, leaks rolling in
items.snark: NFL treating schedule release like the Oscars, just tell us when we play the Jets so we can plan the parade.
